title: SDL DataShape
description: A DataShape describes the structure, constraints, and meaning of data that flows through the service — request payloads, response bodies, domain entities, events, and value objects. DataShapes are the DL Shape concept in SDL. They are NOT a replacement for JSON Schema, Protobuf, or Zod — they are a plain-language description layer that references those formal contracts. DataShapes let engineers understand what data means without reading the formal schema.

dl.notes: DataShape describes the data that flows into/out of Operations (Units) and along Connects. It references formal schemas but does not replace them.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this data shape within the service.

label.description: Human-readable name for this data shape.

description.description: What this data shape represents and where it appears in the service.

kind.description: The role this data shape plays in the service lifecycle.

schema_ref.description: Optional reference to a formal schema that defines this shape precisely. SDL describes it; the contract file defines it.

ref.description: Path or URI to the formal schema. For protobuf, may include the message name after '#'.

fields.description: The fields in this data shape. Write in plain language — not code syntax. Include what each field means and any important business-level constraints.

name.description: Field name as it appears in the data.

type.description: The data type. Use primitives (string, number, boolean, uuid, timestamp), a reference to another DataShape id, or array notation ([]).

required.description: Whether this field must be present.

description.description: What this field means in business terms. Not a technical description — explain the domain significance.

constraints.description: Business-level constraints on this field. Write in plain language, not regex or schema syntax.

pii.description: Whether this field contains personally identifiable information. Affects logging, storage, and compliance requirements.

sensitive.description: Whether this field is sensitive but not strictly PII (e.g., payment amounts, internal IDs, pricing).
